content:
# John Chapter 4 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_004_page_001.jpg, JHN_004_page_002.jpg, JHN_004_page_003.jpg

### Jesus and the Samaritan Woman

1. Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard he was baptizing and making more disciples than John

2. (though Jesus himself didn't baptize anyone — his disciples did).

3. So he left Judea and headed back to Galilee.

4. He had to go through Samaria on the way.

5. He came to the Samaritan village of Sychar, near the plot of land that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.

6. Jacob's well was there, and Jesus, tired from the long walk, sat down beside the well. It was about noon.

7. Soon a Samaritan woman came to draw water, and Jesus said to her, "Please give me a drink."

8. (He was alone at the time because his disciples had gone into the village to buy food.)

9. The woman was surprised. "You are a Jew, and I am a Samaritan woman. Why are you asking me for a drink?" (For Jews refused to have anything to do with Samaritans.)

10. Jesus replied, "If you only knew the gift God has for you and who you are speaking to, you would ask me, and I would give you living water."

11. "But sir, you don't have a rope or a bucket," she said, "and this well is very deep. Where would you get this living water?

12. Besides, do you think you're greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us this well? He drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock."

13. Jesus replied, "Anyone who drinks this water will soon become thirsty again.

14. But those who drink the water I give will never be thirsty again. The water I give becomes a fresh, bubbling spring within them, giving them eternal life."

15. "Please, sir," the woman said, "give me this water! Then I'll never be thirsty again, and I won't have to come here to get water."

16. "Go get your husband," Jesus told her.

17. "I don't have a husband," the woman replied. Jesus said, "You're right — you don't have a husband.

18. The fact is, you've had five husbands, and the man you're living with now isn't your husband. You told the truth."

19. "Sir," the woman said, "you must be a prophet.

20. So tell me — our ancestors worshiped God on this mountain, but you Jews claim that Jerusalem is the only place to worship."

21. Jesus replied, "Believe me, dear woman, the time is coming when it will no longer matter whether you worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem.

22. You Samaritans worship what you don't know. We Jews worship what we do know, for salvation comes through the Jews.

23. But the time is coming — and it's already here — when true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth. The Father is looking for people who will worship him that way.

24. God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in spirit and in truth."

25. The woman said, "I know the Messiah is coming — the one who is called Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us."

26. Then Jesus told her, "I am the one. You don't have to wait any longer."

27. Just then his disciples came back. They were shocked to find him talking to a woman, but none of them had the nerve to ask, "What are you doing?" or "Why are you talking to her?"

28. The woman left her water jar beside the well and ran back to the village, telling everyone,

29. "Come and meet a man who told me everything I ever did! Could he possibly be the Messiah?"

30. So the people came streaming from the village to see him.

31. Meanwhile, the disciples were urging Jesus, "Rabbi, eat something."

32. But Jesus replied, "I have food you know nothing about."

33. "Did someone bring him food while we were gone?" the disciples asked each other.

34. Then Jesus explained: "My food is to do the will of the one who sent me and to finish his work.

35. You know the saying, 'Four months between planting and harvest.' But I say, look around you! The fields are already ripe for harvest.

36. The harvesters are paid good wages, and the fruit they harvest is people brought to eternal life. What joy awaits both the planter and the harvester alike!

37. For it's true that one person plants and someone else harvests.

38. I sent you to harvest where you didn't plant. Others did the hard work, and you have reaped the benefits of their labor."

### Many Samaritans Believe

39. Many Samaritans from that village believed in Jesus because the woman had testified, "He told me everything I ever did."

40. When they came out to see him, they begged him to stay in their village. So he stayed for two days,

41. and many more believed because of his teaching.

42. Then they said to the woman, "Now we believe, not just because of what you told us, but because we have heard him ourselves. Now we know that he really is the Savior of the world."

### Jesus Heals an Official's Son

43. After spending two days there, Jesus went on to Galilee.

44. He himself had said that a prophet is not honored in his own hometown.

45. But the people of Galilee welcomed him, for they had been in Jerusalem at the Passover celebration and had seen everything he did there.

46. He went back to Cana in Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine. A government official in Capernaum had a son who was very sick.

47. When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged Jesus to come to Capernaum to heal his son, who was about to die.

48. Jesus asked, "Will you never believe unless you see signs and wonders?"

49. The official pleaded, "Lord, please come before my little boy dies."

50. Then Jesus told him, "Go back home. Your son will live." The man believed what Jesus said and started home.

51. While he was on his way, his servants met him with the news that his son was alive and well.

52. He asked them when the boy had begun to feel better, and they replied, "Yesterday afternoon at one o'clock his fever suddenly disappeared."

53. Then the father realized that that was the very time Jesus had told him, "Your son will live." And he and his entire household believed.

54. This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did in Galilee after coming from Judea.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 6 — "tired from the long walk":** Jesus is exhausted, thirsty, sitting by a well at noon. The incarnation means real fatigue, real thirst, real human limitation. The one through whom all things were made (1:3) needs a drink of water.

**Verses 16-18 — "Go get your husband":** Jesus doesn't expose her sin to shame her — he reveals what he knows to show her who he is. The woman's history (five husbands, now living with a man who isn't her husband) may also carry symbolic weight: the five husbands could represent the five foreign gods brought to Samaria by the five nations (2 Kings 17:24-34). Whether symbolic or literal, Jesus' knowledge convinces her he is a prophet.

**Verse 24 — "God is spirit":** This is not a definition of God's substance — it's a statement about how God must be approached. The old system required a physical place (temple, mountain). The new reality transcends geography. Wherever there is spirit and truth, there is authentic worship. This verse effectively ends the Gerizim-vs-Jerusalem debate permanently.

**Verse 26 — "I am the one":** In Greek, ἐγώ εἰμι — "I AM." Jesus makes his most explicit self-revelation not to a Jewish leader, not to his disciples, but to a Samaritan woman with a complicated past. The person you'd least expect to receive the revelation receives it first.

**Verse 28 — "She left her water jar":** A tiny detail with huge significance. She came for water. She leaves her jar behind. She found something more satisfying than what she came for. The abandoned jar is a silent symbol of transformation.

**Verse 42 — "Savior of the world":** The Samaritans call Jesus "Savior of the world" — a title used for Roman emperors. These religious outsiders see what Jerusalem's insiders cannot: Jesus' mission is universal, not tribal.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
